seoTitle: How to Create a Task in Jira: Quick Guide
metaDescription: Learn how to create a task in Jira, fill in the key details, assign it to a teammate, and break it into subtasks for better tracking.
intro: Scattering work across chat messages and notes makes it hard to track what's actually getting done. This guide walks through creating a task in Jira, filling in the details that matter, assigning ownership, and breaking bigger work into subtasks.

steps.heading: Open the create issue window
steps.body: Click the Create button in the top navigation to launch the form used for adding new tasks and issues.

steps.heading: Choose your project and issue type
steps.body: Select the project you're working in from the dropdown, then choose Task from the issue type options.

steps.heading: Fill in the task details
steps.body: Type a clear summary title and add a description that explains the work involved so anyone picking it up understands the scope.

steps.heading: Assign and prioritize
steps.body: Select an assignee, set the priority, and add labels to help the task stand out on your board.

steps.heading: Break it into subtasks if needed
steps.body: Open the newly created task and use the add child work item option to split larger deliverables into smaller, trackable pieces.

faq.question: How do I create a task in Jira?
faq.answer: Click Create, choose your project, select Task as the issue type, fill in a summary and description, then click Create to add it to your board.

faq.question: Can I create a task directly from the backlog view?
faq.answer: Yes, most backlog and board views include a quick-add field that lets you create tasks inline without opening the full create form.

faq.question: How do I assign a task to a specific team member?
faq.answer: In the task creation form, select the assignee field and choose the person who should own the work.

faq.question: Can a task have subtasks in Jira?
faq.answer: Yes, once a task is created you can add child work items to break it down into smaller, trackable pieces.
